title: Nostra Operations Space Ontology
description: Example Space-local ontology for incidents, runbooks, and operational mitigations.

classes.description: Sovereign container for policies, contributions, and knowledge bundles.
Governed unit of knowledge, work, or evidence that can be linked and versioned.
Typed link between two governed graph subjects.
A declared ability or feature exposed by a Space or contribution surface.
Named graph scope used to partition evidence and traversal.
An operational event that requires investigation, mitigation, or escalation.
classes.notes: Space-local additive extension for operations tracking.

classes.description: A governed remediation or recovery procedure for an operational scenario.
classes.notes: Represents procedural guidance bound to operational capability surfaces.

relations.description: A Contribution relates to another Contribution.
A Contribution is scoped by a ProvenanceScope.
An incident is mitigated by a runbook.
An incident escalates to another operational contribution.

properties.description: Canonical portable identifier for a graph subject or relation.
Authority ceiling for a bundled artifact or query.
Optional scope-specific reference required operationally for actor and agent scopes.
The operational severity attached to an incident.
The recovery target or service restored by a runbook.

extension_rules: Operations Space terms must remain namespace-prefixed under ops: and additive to the core ontology.
Operations Space extensions must not redefine Space, Contribution, Relation, Capability, or ProvenanceScope.
Breaking changes to operations-space semantics require steward review and an explicit compatibility note.
Operations Space extensions must not add new provenance scope classes or values.
